option.name: updatedContent
option.value: { "change_id": "OBS-TEST-001", "title": "Add full Opik observability to all pipeline stages and refactor tests to single-criterion assertions with difficulty labels", "description": "The agent-runner pipeline currently has minimal Opik instrumentation — only the SDK evaluator is traced. This story adds comprehensive observability across every pipeline stage using Opik's full tracing feature set (spans, metadata, tags, thread IDs, cost tracking) so operators have end-to-end visibility into every workflow run. In parallel, the test suite is refactored so every test case asserts exactly one criterion with a name that describes both what is verified and how hard the fix is for an agent (easy / medium / hard). The dual improvement makes both runtime behavior and test failures immediately actionable.", "acceptance_criteria": [ "AC1 — Opik stage spans: Every pipeline step function in steps.py (step_intake, step_task_gen_producer, step_task_gen_evaluator, step_task_assigner, step_implementation, step_implementation_evaluator, step_qa, step_qa_evaluator, step_lessons_optimizer) is decorated with @opik.track. Each span sets name=<stage-name>, type='tool', and adds metadata containing at minimum: change_id, uow_id (where applicable), runner, and attempt_number (where applicable).", "AC2 — Opik workflow threads: Every trace created during a single workflow run is linked to the same thread_id equal to the change_id string. This groups all spans for a single story run into one Thread in the Opik dashboard, enabling end-to-end visibility. The thread_id is set via opik_context.update_current_trace(thread_id=change_id) inside each tracked stage function.", "AC3 — Opik trace tags and run metadata: Each trace is tagged with the runner name (e.g. tags=['claude'] or tags=['gemini']) and the pipeline stage name (e.g. tags=['stage:intake']). A metadata dict containing {'change_id': ..., 'runner': ..., 'stage': ...} is attached to every trace so dashboard filters work without opening individual trace details.", "AC4 — Opik cost and token tracking: The call_evaluator_sdk function in opik_integration.py attaches token usage data to its span via opik_context.update_current_span(usage={...}) when the LLM response provides usage metadata. For Anthropic responses this is response.usage; for Gemini responses this is response.usage_metadata. Absence of usage data must not raise an exception — the field is omitted gracefully.", "AC5 — Opik evaluator loop spans: The run_eval_optimizer_loop and run_uow_eval_loop functions in run.py are decorated with @opik.track so attempt-level retry loops are individually visible in the Opik timeline. Each span includes metadata: {'change_id': ..., 'stage': ..., 'attempt': ...}.", "AC6 — Single-criterion tests: Every test method in tests/ asserts exactly one thing — one field value, one behaviour, one error condition, or one schema rule. Any test that currently asserts multiple unrelated things is split into one test per assertion. No test method body may contain more than one independent assertion chain (setUp helper assertions excluded).", "AC7 — Descriptive test names with difficulty labels: Every test method name follows the pattern test_<difficulty>__<what_is_verified> where <difficulty> is one of easy, medium, or hard, and <what_is_verified> is a snake_case phrase that uniquely describes the single thing being checked. Example: test_easy__story_yaml_has_change_id_field, test_medium__raw_input_preserves_original_fixture_json, test_hard__full_workflow_routes_gemini_runner_through_all_stages.", "AC8 — Difficulty classification rules documented: A comment block at the top of each test file defines the difficulty rubric used for that file: easy = single-field presence or value equality on a pre-loaded artifact; medium = schema validation, multi-field cross-checks, or mock setup required; hard = multi-stage workflow orchestration, subprocess call verification, or conditional runner behavior." ], "examples": [ "Before: def test_story_has_canonical_schema(self): for field in required_fields: self.assertIn(field, self.story, ...) — fails with 'missing field X' but the test name gives no hint which field or why it matters.", "After: def test_easy__story_yaml_has_change_id_field(self): self.assertIn('change_id', self.story) — name tells you the field, the difficulty tells you the fix is trivial (just add the field).", "Before: test_full_synthetic_workflow_end_to_end verifies AC1 + AC2 + AC3 in one 80-line method — a failure anywhere in the chain is ambiguous.", "After: three separate tests — test_medium__intake_story_yaml_contains_raw_input_section, test_medium__intake_raw_input_preserves_original_fixture_object, test_easy__config_project_type_equals_synthetic_fixture.", "Opik span example: @opik.track(name='stage:intake', type='tool') def step_intake(intake_source, repo, change_id, intake_mode, runner, **kwargs): opik_context.update_current_trace(thread_id=change_id, tags=[runner, 'stage:intake'], metadata={'change_id': change_id, 'runner': runner, 'stage': 'intake'})" ], "constraints": [ "Do not remove or alter any test logic — only split multi-assertion tests and rename them. Existing pass/fail behaviour must be preserved for all currently-passing tests.", "The @opik.track decorator must not change the function signature or return value of any instrumented function. All existing callers must continue to work unchanged.", "Thread ID must equal the change_id string exactly — no hashing, no suffixes. This is the correlation key operators will use to find a run in the dashboard.", "Do not add opik imports to files that do not already import from opik_integration or opik. Add tracing to steps.py and run.py by importing opik at the top of those files.", "The difficulty label is part of the test *method* name, not the test *class* name. Class names remain unchanged.", "Token usage attachment (AC4) must be wrapped in a try/except or checked via hasattr — never assume the attribute exists on the response object.", "Do not add Opik spans to test code. Tracing is for production pipeline code only. Tests should set OPIK_TRACK_DISABLE=true (or equivalent) to suppress trace emission during test runs." ], "non_functional_requirements": [ "All existing unit tests must continue to pass after the refactor — no regressions.", "The opik.track decorator must be imported at the module level, not inside functions.", "Span names must use the format 'stage:<name>' for pipeline steps and 'loop:<name>' for retry loops to enable consistent dashboard filtering.", "After instrumentation, running the pipeline locally must produce visible traces in the configured Opik project (verified manually — not automatable in unit tests).", "The test rename must not break any existing pytest discovery, fixture loading, or conftest.py references." ], "raw_input_notes": { "purpose": "Improve operational transparency via Opik observability and make test failures immediately actionable via single-criterion test assertions with difficulty labels", "owner": "agent-runner", "difficulty": "medium", "difficulty_rationale": "The Opik instrumentation is mechanical but requires reading opik_integration.py, steps.py, and run.py carefully to instrument every function without breaking signatures. The test refactor requires reading all test files and splitting compound assertions — the logic is not difficult but the volume of changes is moderate and requires careful naming discipline.", "target_files": [ "opik_integration.py", "steps.py", "run.py", "tests/test_steps_and_run.py", "tests/test_intake_artifacts.py", "tests/test_downstream_synthetic_mode.py", "tests/test_workflow_inputs.py", "tests/test_run_cmds.py", "tests/test_run_eval.py", "tests/test_eval_story_checks.py" ] } }
